tagline: Hunt dinosaurs from a monster truck.
elevator_pitch: Dino Hunter is an outdoor adventure experience where groups of four pile into real monster trucks, armed with paintball guns, to hunt life-sized dinosaur targets across rugged terrain. It combines the thrill of monster truck driving with competitive team paintball in a Jurassic Park-inspired setting.
problem: Monster truck rallies are declining in popularity because they are purely spectator events with no audience participation. Meanwhile, traditional entertainment like go-karts and paintball have grown stale with little innovation in decades.
solution: Dino Hunter puts you behind the wheel (or behind the paintball gun) of an actual monster truck in an outdoor arena filled with dinosaur targets. Teams compete to outscore each other in timed rounds, combining driving, shooting, and pure adrenaline into one unforgettable outing.
target_market: Adventure-seeking adults aged 18-45, corporate team-building groups, bachelor/bachelorette parties, and families with older kids looking for unique weekend entertainment in suburban and rural markets.
revenue_model: Per-group session pricing ($150-250 per group of four for a 20-30 minute run), premium packages with extended time or exclusive arenas, corporate event bookings, merchandise sales, and seasonal themed events (Zombie Hunter at Halloween, Alien Hunter in summer).
key_features: Drive real monster trucks across rugged outdoor terrain with no special license required
Competitive team paintball scoring against life-sized animatronic and static dinosaur targets
Rotating themed courses and seasonal variants like Zombie Hunter and Alien Hunter
Group packages for corporate events, parties, and team-building with spectator viewing areas
competitive_advantage: No direct competitor combines monster truck driving with paintball gaming in a themed outdoor arena. The concept leverages underutilized rural land, requires no special licensing for participants, and has built-in franchise scalability since the format is portable and repeatable across locations.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. amusement and theme park industry generates over $30 billion annually. Adventure and experiential entertainment (escape rooms, axe throwing, go-karts) is a $5B+ segment growing at 10% per year. Dino Hunter targets even a small slice of this with a TAM of $500M-$1B across 200+ potential U.S. locations.
go_to_market: Launch a flagship location in a suburban market with cheap land and strong family demographics. Build buzz through social media video content (monster trucks + paintball is inherently viral). Partner with corporate event planners and local tourism boards for initial bookings.
risks: High upfront capital for monster trucks, land, and dinosaur props/targets
Liability and insurance costs for letting untrained drivers operate large vehicles
Seasonal limitations in cold-weather markets could reduce operating months
year_one_milestones: Open flagship location and achieve 5,000 bookings in the first 12 months
Generate $750K+ in revenue with 60%+ gross margins on session pricing
Secure letters of intent for 3 franchise locations based on flagship success
